mission.title: Install and Configure Linode Kubernetes Engine (LKE)
mission.description: Linode Kubernetes Engine (LKE) is a managed Kubernetes service that simplifies the deployment and management of Kubernetes clusters on Linode's infrastructure. It is ideal for users looking for a hassle-free way to run Kubernetes without managing the underlying hardware. Key trade-offs include less control over the underlying infrastructure compared to self-managed solutions and potential cost implications based on usage.

steps.title: Install the Linode CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Linode CLI, which is required to interact with the Linode API for creating and managing Kubernetes clusters.

```bash

curl -O https://linode.com/cli/linode-cli-1.23.0-linux-amd64.tar.gz

tar -xzf linode-cli-1.23.0-linux-amd64.tar.gz

sudo mv linode-cli /usr/local/bin/

linode-cli configure

```

> Download the Linode CLI tarball.
> Extract the downloaded tarball.
> Move the CLI to a directory in your PATH.
> Configure the CLI with your Linode API token.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster on Linode with the specified version, node count, and region.

```bash

linode-cli k8s cluster-create --label my-k8s-cluster --region us-east --node-count 3 --k8s-version 1.30

```

> Replace 'my-k8s-cluster' with your desired cluster name.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig file to connect to the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

linode-cli k8s kubeconfig-view my-k8s-cluster > ~/.kube/config

```

> Ensure you have the correct permissions to write to ~/.kube/config.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with a status of 'Ready'.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step sets up additional configurations such as autoscaling, monitoring, and RBAC for your cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/linode/linode-cloud-controller-manager/v0.9.5/deploy/ccm-linode-template.yaml

```

> This applies the necessary configurations for the Linode Cloud Controller Manager.

uninstall.title: Delete the Kubernetes Cluster
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Kubernetes cluster and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss and orphaned resources such as load balancers and persistent volumes.

```bash

linode-cli k8s cluster-delete my-k8s-cluster

```

> Replace 'my-k8s-cluster' with your cluster name.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Kubernetes Cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any important data. This step upgrades the cluster to the latest version.

```bash

linode-cli k8s cluster-update my-k8s-cluster --k8s-version 1.30

kubectl get nodes

```

> Replace 'my-k8s-cluster' with your cluster name.
> Verify that all nodes are in 'Ready' status after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error indicates that the kubeconfig file may not be set up correctly or the cluster is not reachable.

**Fix:**
Re-run the kubeconfig setup command: linode-cli k8s kubeconfig-view my-k8s-cluster > ~/.kube/config.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'No resources found in default namespace'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that there are no resources deployed in the default namespace.

**Fix:**
Check if you have deployed any applications or switch to the correct namespace using 'kubectl get all -n <namespace>'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create service: Service is invalid'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error may occur if there are issues with the service configuration.

**Fix:**
Review the service manifest for errors and ensure all required fields are correctly specified.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources available'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the cluster does not have enough resources to schedule the pod.

**Fix:**
Scale up the cluster by adding more nodes or increasing the resources of existing nodes.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new node types and improved integration with Linode's networking features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required when upgrading from 1.29.

resolution.summary: You should see a list of nodes with 'Ready' status when running 'kubectl get nodes'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the Linode CLI installed and configured with your API token.
